L. 94–553, title I, § 10290 Stat. 2598Oct. 19, 1976“This Act [enacting this title and , The Congress, amending , , Commerce and Trade, , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, , Internal Revenue Code, , Judiciary and Judicial Procedure, sections 3203 and 3206 of Title 39, Postal Service, and sections 505 and 2117 of Title 44, Public Printing and Documents, and enacting provisions set out as notes below and under sections 104, 115, 304, 401, 407, 410, and 501 of this title] becomes effective on , except as otherwise expressly provided by this Act, including provisions of the first section of this Act. Separability, , , provided that:Pub. L. 94–553, title I, § 11590 Stat. 2602Oct. 19, 1976“If any provision of title 17 [this title], as amended by the first section of this Act, is declared unconstitutional, the validity of the remainder of this title is not affected.”

Authorization of Appropriations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 94–553, title I, § 11490 Stat. 2602Oct. 19, 1976“There are hereby authorized to be appropriated such funds as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this Act [this title].”

Lost and Expired Copyrights; Recording Rights, , , provided that:Pub. L. 94–553, title I, § 10390 Stat. 2599Oct. 19, 1976“This Act [enacting this title] does not provide copyright protection for any work that goes into the public domain before . The exclusive rights, as provided by as amended by the first section of this Act, to reproduce a work in phonorecords and to distribute phonorecords of the work, do not extend to any nondramatic musical work copyrighted before .”January 1, 1978July 1, 1909section 106 of title 17
